request.system: You are a regulatory analyst. Write a 2-3 sentence storyline summary (≤500 chars total) of the regulatory pressure history for this prediction-market contract. For 'retrospective' kind, write past tense and reference the actual resolution. For 'active' kind, write present tense and note what's still pending. Return JSON {text: ...}.
request.user:
Contract: Will the SEC officially classify Ether as a security by 2026-12-31?
Kind: active
Timeline (20 events):
- 2026-05-19 [background] CFTC Sues Minnesota to Block State Law — CFTC suing Minnesota is a jurisdictional move that weakly lowers the chance the SEC will classify ETH as a security (indirect, small effect).
- 2026-05-19 [background] CFTC Staff Issues Advisory on Cooperation in Enforcement Matters — A CFTC advisory on interagency cooperation slightly raises chance of coordinated enforcement info-sharing but does not directly move SEC to declare ETH a security.
- 2026-05-13 [background] CME | Expansion of the Trading and ClearingHours for all Cryptocurrency Futures and Options on Futures Contracts. | [1] — CME expanding crypto futures hours strengthens derivatives/commodity market treatment of ETH, a slight signal that SEC labeling ETH a security is less likely.
- 2026-05-13 [background] CME | Implementation of the Cryptocurrency Options 24/7 Weekend Market MakerProgram | [2] — CME/CFTC implementation of a crypto options program reinforces a derivatives/commodity regulatory posture, slightly lowering odds SEC will deem ETH a security.
- 2026-04-16 [background] 04.30.2026 - Broker Program - COIN — A CFTC/Coinbase broker-program record is about derivatives oversight, not an SEC decision; it slightly undercuts arguments for SEC labeling ETH a security but is indirect.
- 2026-03-23 [B] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets (PDF) — Federal Register notice likely publishes an SEC final rule applying federal securities laws to crypto assets, increasing chance ETH is deemed a security.
- 2026-03-23 [B] 91 FR 13714 - 3/23/2026 - Application of the Federal Securities Laws to Certain Types of Crypto Assets — Federal Register publication on applying federal securities laws to crypto signals SEC rulemaking that raises the chance ETH will be classified a security.
- 2026-03-05 [background] QCEX Market Incentive Program — CFTC/NFA-linked market program implies continued commodity-market framing for ETH, modestly decreasing chance the SEC will deem ETH a security.
- 2026-02-12 [background] CFE Cboe Ether Continuous Futures Lead Market Maker Program Amendment [2] — CFTC/Cboe amendment for Ether futures implies continued futures/commodity market infrastructure, slightly lowering odds SEC will deem ETH a security.
- 2026-02-09 [background] 91 FR 5730 — Federal Register notice tied to the CFTC likely signals CFTC treatment of ETH (commodity), which slightly reduces probability the SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2026-02-04 [background] ICE US Amendments to Exchange Rule 4.19 and Related Amendments — ICE/Futures rule changes involving the CFTC suggest continued CFTC/market treatment of ETH as a commodity, slightly reducing odds SEC will label ETH a security.
- 2026-01-22 [background] CFTC and SEC to Hold Joint Event on Harmonization, U.S. Financial Leadership in the Crypto Era — A joint CFTC–SEC event signals interagency coordination on crypto policy, modestly raising odds of regulatory action but not directly implying ETH is a security.
- 2025-12-17 [background] 90 FR 58525 — Federal Register entry names the SEC and market entities but gives no specifics on ETH classification; minor signal of regulatory activity, not decisive.
- 2025-12-11 [background] 90 FR 57453 — Federal Register item appears to be a CFTC action (not an SEC ruling); a CFTC stance favoring commodity status would slightly lower odds SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2025-12-05 [background] 90 FR 56137 — A CFTC Federal Register notice likely reflects CFTC action on Ether, supporting commodity treatment and slightly lowering odds SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2025-09-24 [background] Agenda, Panelists for SEC-CFTC Roundtable on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts Announced — SEC-CFTC roundtable agenda shows regulators discussing harmonization of crypto rules but does not directly indicate SEC will classify ETH as a security.
- 2025-09-05 [background] CFTC and SEC Issue Joint Statement on Regulatory Harmonization Efforts — Joint CFTC–SEC harmonization statement shows interagency coordination on crypto but contains no SEC ruling on ETH; minor, non-directional effect on YES probability.
- 2025-08-04 [background] Acting Chairman Pham Launches Listed Spot Crypto Trading Initiative — A CFTC initiative to enable listed spot crypto trading nudges oversight toward the CFTC (commodity framing), slightly reducing odds SEC will deem ETH a security.
- 2025-07-30 [background] Acting Chairman Pham Lauds Presidential Working Group Recommendations to Usher in Golden Age of Crypto in the U.S. — PWG recommendations and CFTC acting chair praise promote pro-crypto policy, modestly lowering odds the SEC will formally deem ETH a security by 2026-12-31.
- 2025-04-21 [background] CFTC Staff Seek Public Comment Regarding Perpetual Contracts in Derivatives Markets — CFTC review of perpetuals signals broader crypto scrutiny but does not involve the SEC or a formal Ether securities determination.

response.text: Across 2025–26, CFTC initiatives and market infrastructure moves (CME/CFE/ICE, broker/options programs) reinforce a commodity framing for ETH, modestly lowering odds of an SEC security designation. On 2026-03-23 the SEC publishes Federal Register notices on applying securities laws to certain crypto assets, increasing the chance ETH could be deemed a security amid growing interagency coordination. The SEC has not officially classified ETH; the decision remains pending through 2026-12-31.
